regulation.full_name: FDA 21 CFR Part 11 - Electronic Records and Signatures

source.title: Electronic Records; Electronic Signatures
source.disclaimer: Pharmaceutical and medical device companies must consult FDA.gov for complete compliance guidance.

sections.title: Controls for closed systems
sections.text: Persons using closed systems must employ procedures and controls designed to ensure authenticity, integrity, and confidentiality of electronic records. Requirements include: (a) Validation of systems; (b) Audit trails; (c) Operational system checks; (d) Authority checks; (e) Device checks; (f) Education and training; (g) Accountability; (h) Documentation controls; (i) Copying controls.

sections.title: Controls for open systems
sections.text: Persons using open systems must employ additional procedures and controls including document encryption and use of digital signatures to ensure authenticity, integrity, and confidentiality of electronic records.

sections.title: Signature manifestations
sections.text: Signed electronic records must contain information associated with the signing including: (a) Printed name of signer; (b) Date and time when signature was executed; (c) Meaning of signature.

sections.title: Signature/record linking
sections.text: Electronic signatures and handwritten signatures executed to electronic records shall be linked to their respective electronic records to ensure that signatures cannot be excised, copied, or transferred to falsify an electronic record.

sections.title: General requirements for electronic signatures
sections.text: Each electronic signature shall be unique to one individual and shall not be reused by, or reassigned to, anyone else. Before organization establishes, assigns, or certifies electronic signatures, it shall verify the identity of the individual.
